source_pr.title: *: global runaway watch by system table and impl exector for `query watch`
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/43691

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?
1. global runaway watch by system table 
> create table `mysql.tidb_runaway_watch` and table `mysql.tidb_runaway_watch_done` to persist runaway watch and deletion of runaway watch. And all TiDb servers read both tables periodically to sync runaway watch among TiDBs.
2. impl exector for query watch

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
About runaway watch sync:
Execute query on TiDB1, and then execute query on TiDB2
![image](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/assets/23399268/c09b51f1-6e8c-4af0-a245-2a312d8c31a0)
We can see TiDB1 returns error 8253, and TiDB2 returns error 8254. And mysql.tidb_runaway_watch has the record.
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: fix data race | tk.MustQuery("select SQL_NO_CACHE resource_group_name, watch_text, action, watch from mysql.tidb_runaway_watch order by id"). (executor/internal/querywatch/query_watch_test.go)
failure_signature: fix data race | tk.MustQuery("select SQL_NO_CACHE resource_group_name, watch_text, action, watch from mysql.tidb_runaway_watch order by id"). (executor/internal/querywatch/query_watch_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The patch includes an explicit data-race fix in runaway watch synchronization: operations like RemoveRunawayWatch and system-table readers access shared syncer state concurrently. The fix adds proper locking (mu.Lock/Unlock) and avoids unsafe concurrent mutation (e.g., checkpoint updates when doing point reads by ID), making the background sync and manual operations thread-safe.
fix_pattern: Protect runaway watch syncer state with a mutex and avoid unsafe concurrent checkpoint mutation in system-table readers.

evidence: evidence: fix data race | tk.MustQuery("select SQL_NO_CACHE resource_group_name, watch_text, action, watch from mysql.tidb_runaway_watch order by id"). (executor/internal/querywatch/query_watch_test.go)
